text: Csikszentmihalyi's research on flow — the state of complete absorption in a challenging task — reveals a paradox of creative work: the conditions that produce flow also make the work invisible to the worker. In flow, self-monitoring diminishes, time distorts, and effort feels effortless. But these same features mean a creator immersed in execution often cannot evaluate what they have made. Distance is not a luxury; it is an epistemological requirement. The architect who sleeps on a blueprint and finds obvious flaws in the morning has not grown smarter — they have regained the perspective that proximity had erased.
question: Why, according to the passage, can a creator immersed in flow not evaluate their work accurately?

options.text: Self-monitoring diminishes during flow, removing the perspective needed for evaluation.
Flow activates the default mode network, which suppresses critical judgment.
The effortlessness of flow produces overconfidence in the final product.
Csikszentmihalyi found that flow states last too briefly to allow reflection.
